endpoints.name: Pollution Pokemon (Game Freak three-line urban-pollution design cohort)

description: Edgelord here. Stepped aside. Mr. Nodeley apologizes for the inconvenience. The 1:1 Trubbish-Alolan-Grimer predator-prey edge was on the table and sourced, but it's the wrong shape — that's a per-pair ecological-interaction detail that lives in card-body prose under the existing Alolan Grimer Connections section already. The structural move was the cohort node. Six anchors across four species (three lines: Grimer/Muk + Koffing/Weezing + Trubbish/Garbodor) with a designer-stated canonical citation in Garbodor RBC-118 trivia (Sugimori/Unno Nintendo Dream interview articulating the three-substrate framework: liquid sludge / airborne gas / solid waste, all pure Poison-type, all single-evolution, cross-generational deliberate parallel). Citation tier is secondary-aggregator (lavacutcontent.com + remptongames.com surfacing the Nintendo Dream content); flagged in caveats. Potion Rule passes — this is Game Freak's stated design framework, not BBL squinting. Cross-link to alolan-regional-forms via related_characters covers the Alolan Grimer-Trubbish predation overlap as ecological-interaction detail rather than cohort-predicate axis.
citations: [Garbodor RBC-118 Trivia: Sugimori/Unno Nintendo Dream interview design-cohort statement via lavacutcontent.com 'Gen 5 Historia' + remptongames.com 'Evolution of Pokemon Design Gen 5']
[Bulbapedia: Grimer / Muk / Koffing / Weezing / Trubbish / Garbodor per-species design-source sections]
[PokemonTCG.io per-card flavor_text fields canonicalizing per-species pollution-coding: swsh2-117/118/112, swsh3-101, swsh4-110, swsh6-095]
[Alolan Grimer CSE-130 Trivia: Trubbish-predation cross-cohort ecological-interaction citation via Bulbapedia]

next_pass_suggestions.rationale: Available now that the cohort exists; the predation is canonical (Alolan Grimer CSE-130 trivia already cites it), but the connection currently lives only in Alolan Grimer's body. A mirror-edge that explicitly states the predator-prey relationship in BOTH Trubbish prints' Connections sections (mirroring the existing Alolan Grimer CSE-130 statement) would be a clean 1:1 Edgelord-default pass.
next_pass_suggestions.shape: 1:1 mirrored across Trubbish RBC + VIV ↔ Alolan Grimer CSE-130; the Alolan Grimer side already has the statement so this is effectively writing the Trubbish-side mirror

next_pass_suggestions.rationale: Grimer DAA-100 and BRS-084 are vision-passed but trivia-pending. On trivia-pass they attach to pollution-pokemon (and likely also reinforce the existing Grimer/Alolan Grimer regional-variant pairing). Cohort projected to land at 8 prints across 5 species on Grimer completion.
next_pass_suggestions.shape: future trivia-pass + frontmatter attachment, no Edgelord pass needed if triviabot writes characters: [pollution-pokemon] at trivia-pass time

next_pass_suggestions.candidate: Trevenant kodama (tree-spirit yokai) for possible yokai-Pokemon cohort
next_pass_suggestions.rationale: Surfaced wave 103 alongside Nuzleaf-tengu. Currently ≤2 anchors per kodama-spirit framing; below 3-species threshold. Watch for next yokai-coded Pokemon trivia-pass to clear the threshold.

next_pass_suggestions.candidate: Arcanine 'supposed to be Legendary' flavor anchor
next_pass_suggestions.rationale: Flavor-anchor curiosity rather than structural cohort. Insufficient for a node and no clear 1:1 edge target without a sibling 'unrealized-Legendary' anchor.

potion_rule_check.canonical_evidence_for_cohort_predicate: Sugimori/Unno Nintendo Dream interview articulating the three-substrate structural-parallel framework explicitly (designer-stated cross-line program, not BBL squinting); per-species Bulbapedia design-source sections + per-card PokemonTCG.io flavor_text fields cross-confirm the substrate-coding at the printed-card level for all six cohort species
potion_rule_check.citation_tier_concern: Nintendo Dream interview reached the corpus via two secondary aggregators (lavacutcontent.com + remptongames.com), both WebSearch-snippet-tier rather than primary. Flagged in node caveats; future enrichment should target primary archive citation. The cohort thesis stands on convergent secondary citation + structural in-game evidence at all six per-species levels.
potion_rule_check.curatorial_overlay_vs_canonical_event_di: Node body explicitly distinguishes canonical-event (Game Freak's species-design choice, Sugimori/Unno designer-statement) from curatorial-overlay (BBL's reading of cohort as 'species-as-pollution-body'). Phrases like 'the franchise's clearest articulation of' are gated on 'BBL reads this canonical-event as...' constructions per Potion Rule sub-rule 1.
potion_rule_check.no_new_structural_primitive_vocabulary_i: Standard graph-theory terms used: cohort (=node), appears_on (=edge-list), related_characters (=cross-link). No invented sub-shape jargon. Potion Rule sub-rule 4 held.
